description: Bug: the product page does not load its micro-frontend. The 'store' remote (product list at /store and product detail at /store/product/:id) is the page in question; the host loads it at runtime via @module-federation/runtime init()+loadRemote('store/ConsoleMicroApp') from {remoteUrl}/remoteEntry.js (dev remoteUrl=http://localhost:4201) in host/src/microFe/MicroFrontendApp.tsx, and a load/mount failure renders ErrorBoundaryFallback ('Failed to Load Micro Frontend'). DONE-DEFINITION: navigating to /store in the host renders the store remote (no ErrorBoundary, spinner resolves) and both the product list and a product detail page render with data; the fix is verified in the browser and guarded against regression. The union of the stories below fully satisfies this.

userStories.title: Reproduce and root-cause the product-page MFE load failure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the exact failure of the product page captured and root-caused so the fix targets the real cause instead of a guess.
acceptanceCriteria: Reproduce by serving the suite (cd micro-frontends && npm run setup && npm start) and opening the host at http://localhost:4200/store
Capture the browser console and Network tab evidence: the request for http://localhost:4201/remoteEntry.js and its status, plus any '[MicroFrontendApp] Error loading store' / ErrorBoundary message

Classify the failing layer with a cited file:line — (a) remote not served/remoteEntry 404 or CORS, (b) loadRemote/share-scope or React/antd singleton error, (c) injector mismatch, or (d) MFE mounts but product data fails from the gateway
Confirm whether the 'store' remote is actually served on :4201 by npm start (nx run-many serve) and whether NX_MF_DEV_REMOTES affects it
Record the root cause and the reproduction steps in progress.txt (Codebase Patterns + a dated block)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser (screenshot of the failing page + console for the progress log)
userStories.notes: Grounded in host/src/microFe/MicroFrontendApp.tsx (runtime init/loadRemote), host/src/config/microFrontendRegistry.ts (dev port 4201, /remoteEntry.js), host/src/helpers/environment.ts (getRemoteUrl), store/module-federation.config.ts (exposes ./ConsoleMicroApp), store/src/remote-entry.ts -> injector.ts. Diagnosis-first because the precise root cause requires the live console/network.

userStories.title: Fix Module Federation loading so the store remote mounts in the host
userStories.description: As a user, I want navigating to /store to load and mount the store micro-frontend so the product page appears instead of an error fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Apply the fix for the root cause identified in US-001 across the load path: host/src/microFe/MicroFrontendApp.tsx (@module-federation/runtime init/loadRemote), host/src/config/microFrontendRegistry.ts (remote URL + /remoteEntry.js), host webpack.config.ts vs store module-federation.config.ts (name 'store', exposes './ConsoleMicroApp'), and dev serve on :4201
Ensure shared singletons (react, react-dom, antd, @tanstack/*) are configured so loadRemote does not throw a share-scope/duplicate-React error

Navigating to http://localhost:4200/store mounts the store remote: the Spin loader resolves, no ErrorBoundaryFallback ('Failed to Load Micro Frontend') is shown, and the injected container (id mfe-store-*) contains the store app
remoteModule.default exposes { inject, unmount } and inject() runs without throwing (store/src/injector.ts)
No regressions to the other remotes (checkout/account/admin) loading via the same MicroFrontendApp path
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser (screenshot of /store mounted for the progress log)
userStories.notes: Most fragile seam: the host loads remotes with @module-federation/runtime manually while the build uses @nx/module-federation — watch share scope / duplicate React. Keep the registry/exposed-module names case-sensitive ('store' / 'ConsoleMicroApp').

userStories.title: Ensure product list and detail render with data once mounted
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want the product list and a product detail page to actually show products so the page is usable, not just mounted.
acceptanceCriteria: /store renders the product grid (store/src/pages/ProductList.tsx) with products fetched from the gateway, and shows correct loading/empty/error states (LoadingState/ErrorState components)
/store/product/:id renders ProductDetail for a valid product id (route store/src/routes/product/$id.tsx)

Data calls hit the gateway via env.apiBaseUrl (NX_API_BASE_URL, default http://localhost:8010) using the /Catalog/* paths (store/src/services/products/apis.ts: GetAllProducts, GetProductById/:id); confirm mock mode is off by default (NX_USE_MOCK_DATA !== 'true')
With the gateway/Catalog service unavailable, the UI shows the error state rather than a blank page or unhandled rejection
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser (screenshots of the product list and a product detail for the progress log)
userStories.notes: Data layer already targets the gateway short-form /Catalog routes (store/src/services/products/apis.ts, httpClient.ts, config/env.config.ts). Requires the backend gateway (:8010) + Catalog service running, or NX_USE_MOCK_DATA=true to validate UI independently. Only needed if loading was the sole symptom — included to guarantee the page is actually usable.

userStories.title: Verify end-to-end and guard against regression
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the product-page flow verified end-to-end and protected by a test so it does not silently break again.
acceptanceCriteria: End-to-end manual pass: home (/) -> navigate to /store -> open a product detail at /store/product/:id, all rendering correctly with screenshots in the progress log
Add or update an automated guard: a Playwright e2e (micro-frontends/e2e) or store route test (store/src/routes/index.test.tsx) asserting the store remote mounts and the product list renders
npm test (nx run-many test) and the e2e guard pass locally
Append final outcome + learnings to progress.txt
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser (final screenshots of the working product list + detail for the progress log)
userStories.notes: Existing test scaffolding: micro-frontends/e2e (Playwright), store/src/routes/index.test.tsx (Jest). Closes the done-definition and prevents regression of the MFE load path.
